title: Synthetic differential-expression matrix (data file)
context: Differential expression analysis is a core bioinformatics skill for cancer data learners, but realistic expression datasets either require controlled-access (e.g. TCGA via dbGaP) or are large enough to be unusable in a 90-minute lesson. A synthetic gene-expression matrix, generated from published aggregate expression characteristics (e.g. published fold-change distributions and variance parameters from open-tier studies), teaches the skill without touching controlled-access data. The data file is the synthetic artifact; the teaching layer is ds-expr-lesson-014. Depends on catalog-010 (approved source for calibration), synthesis-004 (methodology), and tooling-006 (generator harness).
objective: Produce a synthetic differential-expression matrix (genes x samples, two-group design) generated from published aggregate expression characteristics, passing gate-002 with full documentation and a Zenodo DOI.
acceptanceCriteria: Synthetic expression matrix generated from a seed + parameters provenanced to published aggregate expression characteristics; no individual-level data input.
gate-002 PASS artifact committed; safety notice present and accurate; riskTier:low.
Data dictionary covers genes x samples structure + group labels; known limitations and biases documented; completeness >= 90/100; Croissant metadata valid; Zenodo DOI minted.
Reproducibility check = PASS: independent reviewer re-runs seed/params and produces identical file.
outputLicense: CC0-1.0 (or CC-BY-4.0 if calibrated from a CC-BY source with attribution).
Safety reviewer sign-off on gate artifact; technical reviewer confirms data dictionary and reproducibility.
output: A synthetic differential-expression matrix CSV/TSV + provenance.json + gate PASS artifact + Croissant metadata + data dictionary + Zenodo DOI, ready for the ds-expr-lesson-014 teaching layer.
